title: Fifth Battle of Qotile
content:

*Underworld ships[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] **[[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Wayward Son'']] *[[Starfighter/Legends|Starfighters]] |forces3= |forces4= |casual1=*Ranulph Tarkin |civilian= }} {{Quote|They will have the numbers. We will have the Force.|[[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]]|[[Star Wars (1998) 39|''Star Wars'' (1998) 39]]}} The '''Fifth Battle of Qotile''' was the last battle of the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]. ==Background== After the failure of [[Stark Commercial Combine|Stark Collective]] forces in the [[Fourth Battle of Qotile|previous battle]], [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark's]] co-directors were unwilling to continue to tolerate what they saw as his bumbling leadership, and were on the verge of killing him, when he told them that he had an ally on the inside of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] stronghold of [[Mount Avos]]—neglecting to point out, however, that his ally was [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]], who had convinced Stark via [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathy]] to defect. Meanwhile, [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] and the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], on threat of incriminating disclosures to the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]], had given the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] the use of a fleet of [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|freighters]] and [[C-9979 landing craft/Legends|C-9979 landing craft]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Oppo Rancisis/Legends|Oppo Rancisis]] was on the bridge of the command ship, where his use of [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]] would be most effective, while [[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin]] prepared to command the two Jedi fighter groups, [[Red Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Red]] and [[Blue Squadron (Jedi Order)|Blue Squadrons]]. [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], [[Adi Gallia/Legends|Adi Gallia]], and [[Eeth Koth/Legends|Eeth Koth]] also accompanied the force. Although outnumbered by the Combine fleet, they trusted that their better training, resolve, and use of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] would carry the battle. ==The battle== On [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]], Jedi Knight [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] infiltrated the Combine's command ship, [[Raptor (starship)|''Raptor'']], in preparation for broadcasting a patch to the Combine's crippling [[navcomputer virus]], while [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] infiltrated the Combine's headquarters to acquire the patch itself. Jinn and Kenobi encountered trouble, however, as the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] in charge of the patch required a password to release it. The password was soon supplied by Stark himself, who arrived with two of his co-directors—both of whom ([[Boor-Daa]] and [[Tam Gozon]]) he killed in front of the Jedi. Having [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathically]] received from Jedi Knight Plo Koon a promise of amnesty with his cooperation, Stark (having already realized his cause was a lost one) supplied the password and then helped the two Jedi pacify the command center. [[File:Stark_Virus-patch_transmission_comlink_acquisition.jpg|thumb|left|Jedi Master [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and Padawan [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] secure the necessary [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] aboard the [[Stark Commercial Combine|Stark Combine's]] command ship [[Raptor (starship)|''Raptor'']] in order to transmit a virus patch to Coruscant's Jedi-led fleet, which enabled them to defeat the Combine and win the Stark Hyperspace War of 44 BBY.|200x200px]] Overhead, Tholme and Vos transmitted the patch to the Trade Federation fleet, with Vos additionally reversing the controls of the [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield generators]] of the ''Raptor'', setting them instead to lower when ordered to raise. The Jedi then fought their way off the ship on one of its fighters as the Jedi force arrived en masse in the [[Troiken system/Legends|Troiken system]]. At Mount Avos, the [[Outland Regions Security Force/Legends|Republic force]] retreated out a back entrance of the mine while the Combine forces swarmed in from the front. Koon planned to seal both entrances once the Republic soldiers had evacuated, thus trapping the Combine soldiers harmlessly inside the mine. [[File:Troikens_true_hero.jpg|thumb|180px|'''Troiken's true hero''': Jedi Master [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] calls upon the Force to seal the last entrance to the [[Mount Avos]] caves, which won the final battle of the Stark Hyperspace War.]] The plan failed however when, determined to kill off both the Jedi who had stolen his glory and the enemy he had been unable to vanquish, [[General/Legends|General]] [[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]] stole the last detonator charge and used it to open the challat eater cave to a devouring of all forces within the caves, killing himself in the blast while securing for himself a self-styled immortality as the "Hero of Troiken"—the man who indeed destroyed the Stark Combine and won the war in one fell swoop of self-sacrifice. Koon managed to escape just in time, using the Force to seal the mine, unfortunately trapping the majority of the Combine soldiers inside the insect-infested mine, where they were eaten alive. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Fame, recognition—a Jedi does not seek these things. It was enough to serve and have good come from that."
"Maybe for you, Master Plo. But for the rest of us who ''fought'' there, who paid the ''price'' in blood and pain, we know the ''truth''. ''You'', Master Plo Koon, saved our lives. For that, you have our undying respect and ''loyalty'', to our dying day.|Jedi Master [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]] of the Jedi High Council and conflict veteran [[Jace Dallin]] reflect in [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]] on the part Koon played in the Stark Hyperspace War|}} As the ''Raptor'' was destroyed overhead, the few surviving ground forces of the Combine surrendered, ending both the battle and the Stark Hyperspace War. Plo Koon was made a Jedi Master and would hold a lifetime seat on the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]], in honor of [[Tyvokka/Legends|his Master's]] dying wish. Tarkin's family, in a bid to rightfully exalt their progenitor as the "Hero of Troiken," later struck a political bargain with [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]], supporting his election as [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] in [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]] in exchange for securing, via posthumous decoration, Tarkin's reputation as the war's definitive hero. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1998) 39|''Star Wars'' (1998) 39]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *[[Millennium Falcon (novel)|''Millennium Falcon'']] {{Ab|Millennium Falcon (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Episode I Who's Who: A Pocket Guide to the Characters of The Phantom Menace]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' {{1stID|Fifth Battle of Qotile}} *''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]'' *{{StarshipsVehiclesCite|23|Droids, Aliens & Creatures}} *[[Stay on Target (sourcebook)|''Stay on Target'']] ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{StarkHyperspaceWarNav}} {{Interlang |de=Fünfte Schlacht von Troiken |es=Quinta Batalla de Qotile }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Qotile 5}} [[Category:Battles of the Stark Hyperspace War]] [[Category:Battles of Troiken]]
